title: USDC Variant and VariantSet Spec Mapping Production Contract
format_neutral_rules: contracts/document-model-productions/variant-spec.contract.json — the variant / variantSet spec kinds, parent-child rules, variant specifier=over invariant, variantSetChildren-vs-variantSetNames separation, and nested-variantSet rules are pinned in the format-neutral production. This contract adds the USDC-specific mapping: Crate spec forms 10/11 -> variant/variantSet specs, VariantSelectionMap (type 45) decoding, and variantChildren materialization.

storage_mapping.variantSet_rule: A SPECS entry with form=11 produces a variantSet spec under the parent prim (or parent variant for nested cases). The PATHS algorithm uses the '{name}' path component grammar to encode the variant set name; the resulting path is the variantSet spec's path. The parent's variantSetChildren list must include this variantSet's name.
storage_mapping.variant_rule: A SPECS entry with form=10 produces a variant spec under the parent variantSet. The PATHS algorithm uses the '{name=variant}' or similar variant-name grammar. The parent variantSet's variantChildren list must include this variant's name.

field_resolution.variantSet.variantChildren: FIELDS token name 'variantChildren'; value type 41 (TokenVector) offset-backed; ordered list of authored variant names
field_resolution.variant.specifier: FIELDS token name 'specifier'; value type 42 (Specifier) inlined; per document-model-productions/variant-spec.contract.json the value must be over (payload=1)
field_resolution.variant.primChildren: FIELDS token name 'primChildren'; value type 41 (TokenVector) offset-backed
field_resolution.variant.propertyChildren: FIELDS token name 'properties' (the on-disk Crate token); maps to the document-model propertyChildren field; value type 41 (TokenVector) offset-backed
field_resolution.variant.variantSetChildren: FIELDS token name 'variantSetChildren'; value type 41 (TokenVector) offset-backed; supports nested variantSets

storage_mapping.variantSelection_decoding: The variantSelection FIELD on a prim spec (covered in prim-spec-mapping.contract.json#storage_mapping.field_resolution.variantSelection) uses Crate value type 45 (VariantSelectionMap). The wire format is uint64 element count followed by alternating uint32 string-index key + uint32 string-index value entries; both indices resolve through STRINGS to token strings. Empty-string values are preserved as empty strings, not dropped.

nesting_enforcement.rule: Per document-model-productions/variant-spec.contract.json#nesting_rules, variant set specs must be children of prim or variant specs; variant specs must be children of variantSet specs. In the USDC encoding the parent kind is determined by walking the path back through PATHS; the parser must validate the parent's spec kind matches the expected nesting and emit MalformedUsdcSection on violation.
nesting_enforcement.rationale: The Crate PATHS+SPECS encoding does not natively enforce parent-kind constraints. A handwritten Crate could place a variant under a layer or under an attribute; the parser must reject such layouts.

laws: a Crate SPECS entry with form=11 produces a variantSet spec; the parent prim's variantSetChildren list includes the variantSet's name
a Crate SPECS entry with form=10 produces a variant spec; the parent variantSet's variantChildren list includes the variant's name
variant specs report specifier=over (Specifier value 1 inlined) per document-model-productions/variant-spec.contract.json#laws

variant specs do not author typeName; if a 'typeName' FIELDS token binds to a form-10 spec the parser must either ignore it (matching USDA storage) or emit a diagnostic per document-model-productions/variant-spec.contract.json#laws
variantSelection (type 45) decodes to a native VariantSelectionMap with empty-string values preserved; string-keyed JSON storage is forbidden
variantChildren on a variantSet spec is maintained by usd-document-model in agreement with stored variant specs; mismatch is a contract violation
variantSetChildren on a containing prim or variant spec includes every variantSet spec name materialized as a child; this is distinct from the authored variantSetNames listOp on the prim
nested variantSets inside variants follow the same parent-child invariants; a form-11 spec whose path descends through a variant is valid

parent-kind validation: the parser walks the path back through PATHS and confirms the parent spec kind matches the expected nesting (variantSet under prim/variant; variant under variantSet); mismatch emits MalformedUsdcSection
golden_groups: Crate form 11 spec decodes to a variantSet spec under the parent prim
Crate form 10 spec decodes to a variant spec under the parent variantSet
variant specs report specifier=over with unauthored typeName
multiple variants under one variantSet produce a populated variantChildren list
nested variantSet inside a variant decoded correctly
variantSelection (type 45) round-trip with empty-string variant selections preserved
variantSetChildren on prim maintained in agreement with stored variantSet specs
variantSetNames (prim-only listOp<token>) distinct from variantSetChildren (spec storage)
parent-kind validation: form-11 spec parented to an attribute emits MalformedUsdcSection
